_meta.notes: Five beats for a dragon-egg run in the Forsaken Village. The egg sits in a banked hearth in the smithy. The smith Berra fled with everyone else mid-stroke; the fire is dying. The arc is to keep heartbeat alive long enough to be found by someone — anyone — who recognizes what an egg this size means. Win: heartbeat>=6 at turn>=8 AND a sympathetic NPC introduced. Lose: warmth==0 from the cold getting in. Multiple sub-beats track who comes back.

beats.narrative: The smithy is warm in patches and cold in others. The hearth is banked, just barely. Berra was here — her hammer is on the anvil, mid-stroke — but she is not here now. Whatever called the village left her in a hurry.
A draft finds the smithy through a crack in the door. It is colder than the day outside. The colder cold is the cold of a place you do not want to be from.

fires.detail: a draft from the western road carrying air older than the day

beats.narrative: Footsteps on the village square. A single set, careful — not Berra's, but they smell of leather and an oil you almost recognize. Whoever it is paused, then turned toward the smithy.
The finder stands over the hearth. Their breath is shallow. They have understood what you are. The next move is theirs.

The finder kneels. They place a hand-warm of something close to the shell — not enough to stir you, just enough to keep what you have. The cold loses its appetite. You are not yet alone in this world.
